bio: Reza Marashi served as the first Research Director of the [National Iranian American Council (NIAC)](/orgs/niac), joining in 2010. Before NIAC, he spent four years at the U.S. State Department's Office of Iranian Affairs and worked at the Institute for National Strategic Studies on China-Middle East issues. He was also a Tehran-based private strategic consultant connected to the Atieh Bahar consulting ecosystem. His career path — from the State Department's Iran desk to NIAC — exemplifies the revolving door between government Iran policy positions and the NIAC network that critics have highlighted.
editorial_notes: First person to hold NIAC Research Director role. Prior State Dept and Tehran consulting background.
